front: Film: Mookie (played by Lee) throws a trash can through Sal's Pizzeria window, sparking a riot, after Radio Raheem is killed by police
Film: Radio Raheem carries a boombox playing 'Fight the Power' by Public Enemy; Sal destroys it with a baseball bat, leading to Raheem's death
Film: Radio Raheem wears knuckle rings reading 'LOVE' and 'HATE'; delivers 'right hand/left hand' speech homaging The Night of the Hunter
Film: features a racial stereotype insult montage — characters of different ethnicities speaking directly into the camera
Film: ends with mentally disabled Smiley's photo of MLK and Malcolm X shaking hands placed on Sal's Wall of Fame
Film: cinematographer Ernest Dickerson used a heat bar under the camera to distort the image and simulate intense summer heat
Film: Sal's Wall of Fame only had pictures of Italian-Americans; Radio Raheem nicknamed for his boombox
Film: Ron Stallworth impersonates a white nationalist over the phone to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan
Film: biopic starring Denzel Washington and Angela Bassett; costumes by Ruth E. Carter
Film: two friends discuss the future of their friendship at a window overlooking Ground Zero; protagonist reads 'Fuck You' on his father's bar wall and delivers a racial monologue with a city montage
Film: accused of anti-Semitism by the ADL for portraying two club owners as 'Shylocks'; main character buys two women the same dress
Film: directorial debut (1986); Nola Darling cycles between three men and claims monogamy is slavery; remade as Netflix series in 2017
Film: five Black Vietnam veterans return to find gold buried in the 1960s and lost after a napalm strike
Director: popularized the double dolly zoom (from Van Peebles) to represent 'black suspicion of the white gaze'
Director: directed American Utopia (2020) — concert film of David Byrne's Broadway show; opens with overhead shot of Byrne holding a model of a human brain
Director: directed When the Levees Broke: A Requiem in Four Acts — documentary about Hurricane Katrina devastating New Orleans; scored by Terence Blanchard
